title: Agent OS ROI review: concurrency and handoff primitives
goal: Capture the ROI case for lightweight concurrency and handoff primitives.
Keep the review separate from active planning promotion.
scope: Issue #1115 ROI point 7: concurrency and handoff primitives.
Task/file claims, stale-claim recovery, handoff summaries, and last-proven records.
non_goals: Do not introduce lock files or claim protocols in this review.
Do not convert handoff summaries into a standalone package promise.

review_mode.review question: Would lightweight claims and last-proven handoff records reduce restart and multi-agent collision cost?
review_mode.inputs inspected first: Issue #1115 body, planning state active/roadmap shape, docs/agent-os-capabilities.md collaboration and handoff sections

review_method.commands used: uv run agentic-workspace start --task "Create one planning review artifact for each of the ten agent OS capability ROI points from issue #1115" --format json; Get-Content .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml
review_method.evidence sources: GitHub #1115; planning state with no active execplans; docs/agent-os-capabilities.md

references.label: Collaboration / Concurrency Safety and Handoff / Execution Summaries

findings.title: Concurrency and handoff primitives are medium-high ROI supporting infrastructure
findings.summary: The repo already separates active planning, roadmap candidates, and review artifacts, but lightweight claims, stale-claim recovery, handoff summaries, and last-proven records would make multi-agent continuation cheaper without making handoff a standalone product.
findings.evidence: Issue #1115 ranks concurrency/handoff seventh. The current startup output showed no active execplans and gave safe routing for direct work; the planning state keeps active items and roadmap candidates separate. The capability doctrine treats collaboration as internal and handoff as unlikely to stand alone, which matches the supporting-infrastructure shape.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents can collide on high-churn workflow files, lose recent validation context, or resume from chat summaries instead of a compact checked-in last-proven record.
findings.suggested action: Create a child slice only when multi-agent collision or restart failures recur; keep the first design as Planning-owned support with Workspace reporting.
findings.promotion target: GitHub child issue under #1115 or future Planning roadmap candidate
findings.promotion trigger: Promote after repeated stale-claim, handoff, or last-proof reconstruction friction.
findings.post-remediation note shape: stub pointing to the planning support contract

recommendation.promote: Not first; preserve as a later child slice unless concurrency friction becomes urgent.
recommendation.dismiss: Do not dismiss; retain the support-infrastructure framing.

retention.trigger: A child issue or planning support contract captures concurrency/handoff primitives.
retention.proof surface: Planning state, handoff output, and closeout proof records.

validation_commands: uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
drift_log: 2026-05-24: Review record created from issue #1115 ROI point 7.
